headline: Iran tightens Strait of Hormuz grip, oil rises
detail: Iran escalating in the Strait of Hormuz sends oil prices higher, which tightens financial conditions and compresses growth-stock multiples. Your chip and software heavy book — AVGO, PLTR, SMH, CRWD — is directly exposed to any broad risk-off rotation that follows an energy spike.

headline: Trump threatens 100% tariff on Digital Services Taxes
detail: A 100% tariff threat targeting countries taxing American tech companies sounds like a win but historically triggers retaliatory measures and trade uncertainty that freezes enterprise tech spending. AVGO and CRWD both have significant international revenue exposure, and SMH tracks semis that depend heavily on global supply chains.

headline: OpenAI restricts new AI models to trusted partners at U.S. government request
detail: The U.S. government is actively shaping who gets access to frontier AI, adding regulatory overhang to the AI infrastructure buildout narrative. This directly affects the demand story underpinning PLTR (AI government contracts) and AVGO (AI chip/networking), so watch for any market repricing of that growth premium.
